---
summary: 'Adding a new template to the bundled gallery — shape, manifest entry, build pipeline.'
title: 'Authoring a template'
---

# Authoring a template

Adding a template to the bundled gallery means:

1. Build the app as you would any other Centraid app.
2. Drop it under `packages/blueprints/apps/<id>/` (UI app) or `packages/blueprints/automations/<id>/` (automation).
3. Ship the handlers compiled to `.js` so first use needs no `bun install`.
4. Add an entry to `packages/blueprints/index.json`.
5. Regenerate `manifest.json`.

## Steps

### 1. Shape the app

Build the folder using the standard layout — see [App anatomy](/build/app-anatomy). A UI app:

```
packages/blueprints/apps/<id>/
├── app.json
├── index.html
├── app.css
├── app.js
├── queries/
│   └── <name>.js
├── actions/
│   └── <name>.js
├── migrations/
│   └── 0001_init.sql
├── automations/         # optional in-app automations
│   └── <id>/
│       ├── automation.json
│       └── handler.js
└── package.json
```

An automation template lives under `automations/<id>/`, sets `app.json#kind = "automation"`, ships **no** `index.html`, and carries its automation under `automations/<id>/{automation.json, handler.js}`.

The kind-segment directory (`apps/` vs `automations/`) is derived from the template's `kind`, so put the folder under the matching directory.

### 2. Ship compiled `.js` handlers

The bundled gallery promises "click and deploy" — no `bun install`, no compile step. The runtime loads `.js` only, so commit the **compiled `.js`** for every handler. The bundled templates ship plain `.js` and carry no `.ts` source and no `tsconfig.json`.

If you author in TypeScript, compile out-of-tree (a `tsconfig.json` with `"rootDir": "."` / `"outDir": "."` emits `queries/foo.js` next to `queries/foo.ts`) and commit only the emitted `.js`.

### 3. Add a gallery entry

Edit `packages/blueprints/index.json`. For a UI app (`kind` defaults to `'app'` and is omitted):

```json
{
  "id": "your-app-id",
  "name": "Display Name",
  "desc": "One-sentence description.",
  "colorKey": "indigo",
  "iconKey": "Sparkle",
  "version": "0.1.0"
}
```

For an automation template, set `kind` and the card display fields:

```json
{
  "id": "your-automation-id",
  "name": "Display Name",
  "desc": "One-sentence description.",
  "colorKey": "rose",
  "iconKey": "Sparkle",
  "version": "0.1.0",
  "kind": "automation",
  "emoji": "🌤",
  "category": "Daily rhythm",
  "triggerKind": "cron",
  "triggerLabel": "Weekdays · 6:00 PM",
  "integrations": ["Google Calendar", "Gmail"]
}
```

`triggerKind` is `"cron"` or `"webhook"`; `triggerLabel` and `integrations` are free-form display strings shown on the card.

### 4. Regenerate `manifest.json`

`index.json` is hand-edited; the runtime catalog `manifest.json` is generated:

```sh
cd packages/blueprints
bun run build:manifest      # or: bun run build (runs this, then tsc)
```

The script (`scripts/build-manifest.mjs`) walks each template folder, enumerates its files into `files[]` (so the remote fetcher knows what to download), folds each template's `app.json#knobs` into the manifest as `appKnobs`, and writes `manifest.json`. **Commit `manifest.json`** — it's checked in so the same path on GitHub raw can serve as the remote manifest, with no separate publish step.

### 5. Bump the version on subsequent changes

The `version` field follows semver. Bump it (in both `app.json` and the `index.json` gallery entry) whenever the template's source changes — that's what lets the resolver detect that a remote or cached copy supersedes the bundled one (it prefers the higher semver).

## Color and icon keys

`colorKey` and `iconKey` come from `@centraid/design-tokens`.

Color keys (`packages/design-tokens/src/palette.ts`): `amber`, `forest`, `indigo`, `ochre`, `rose`, `slate`, `teal`, `violet`.

Icon keys (`packages/design-tokens/src/icons.ts`) are a larger set; the templates use `Water`, `Todo`, `Journal`, and `Sparkle`. Others include `Calendar`, `Bolt`, `Globe`, `Code`, `Activity`, `Home`, `Book`, and more — see the icon catalog for the full list.

## Categories

Automation templates use `category` to group cards in the gallery. The string is free-form (not a design-tokens enum); the bundled set is:

- `Daily rhythm`
- `Inbox & comms`
- `Engineering`
- `Reliability`
